description: Fix multiple factual errors in README.md that cause first-time users to fail during setup: wrong browser tool count, missing --show-secret flag, wrong MCP client config path, wrong plugin install command, and nonexistent --dev flag.

userStories.title: Fix all factual errors in README.md
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want the README Quick Start instructions to be accurate so that I can successfully set up OpenTabs by following them.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md line 31 says '39 built-in browser tools' instead of '36'
README.md line 98 says '39 built-in browser tools' instead of '36'
README.md line 64 uses 'opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret' (with --show-secret flag) instead of 'opentabs config show --json | jq -r .secret'
README.md line 87 uses 'opentabs plugin install slack' instead of 'npm install -g opentabs-plugin-slack'

README.md lines 122-126 no longer reference 'opentabs start --dev' since that flag does not exist on the CLI; replace with accurate plugin development workflow using 'npm run dev' in the plugin directory
userStories.notes:
File: README.md (198 lines total). Five specific fixes needed:

1. Lines 31 and 98: Change '36 built-in browser tools' to '39 built-in browser tools'. The server health endpoint confirms 39 is the actual count, and all docs pages already say 39.

2. Line 64: Change 'opentabs config show --json | jq -r .secret' to 'opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret'. Without --show-secret, the CLI returns a truncated secret like '8452...c823' which is useless for auth. The docs site (quick-start.mdx, install/index.mdx, troubleshooting.mdx, mcp-server.mdx) already use the correct command with --show-secret.

3. Line 67: Change '~/.claude.json' to '~/.claude/settings/mcp.json'. The CLI's own start command already prints the correct path. The docs site already uses the correct path.

4. Line 87: Change 'npm install -g opentabs-plugin-slack' to 'opentabs plugin install slack'. The CLI resolves shorthand names automatically. Also remove 'opentabs start' from line 88 since the server should already be running from a previous step — the plugin install command notifies the running server via POST /reload. Keep 'opentabs plugin search' on line 86.

5. Lines 122-126: The section 'Develop with hot reload' shows 'opentabs start --dev' but this flag does not exist on the CLI (running it gives 'error: unknown option --dev'). Replace this section with accurate instructions: plugin developers should use 'npm run dev' inside their plugin directory for watch mode (auto-rebuild on changes). The plugin build notifies the running server via POST /reload. The scaffolded plugin already has a 'dev' npm script.
